note_data.title: Functions
keyPoints: Function is a sub-program or sub-routine that performs a specific task and can be called from the main program
Functions reduce program length, avoid repetition of code, and make programs easier to understand and debug
There are two types of functions: Built-in functions (provided by C language) and User-defined functions (created by programmer)
Function declaration tells the compiler about function name, return type, and parameters
Function definition contains the actual code that executes when function is called
Function call is used to execute the function from main program or another function
Return statement sends value back to the calling function
Parameters are values passed to function while arguments are actual values received by function

definitions.definition: A function is a sub-program or sub-routine that performs a specific task and can be called from the main program or from another function
Function declaration tells the compiler about the function name, return type, and parameters but does not contain the function body
Function definition contains the actual statements or code that executes when the function is called
Built-in functions are pre-defined functions provided by C language such as printf(), scanf(), sqrt(), pow() etc.
User-defined functions are created by the programmer according to program requirements to perform specific tasks

importantQuestions.question: Define function and write its advantages.
importantQuestions.answer: Function is a sub-program that performs specific task. Advantages: Reduces program length, avoids code repetition, makes program easier to understand and debug, allows code reusability.

importantQuestions.question: Differentiate between function declaration and function definition. Write a C program using user-defined function to calculate area of circle.
importantQuestions.answer: Function Declaration: Tells compiler about function name, return type and parameters without function body. Function Definition: Contains actual code that executes. Program: #include<stdio.h> float area(float r); int main(){ float radius, result; printf("Enter radius: "); scanf("%f", &radius); result = area(radius); printf("Area = %.2f", result); return 0;} float area(float r){ return 3.14*r*r;}

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a built-in function in C?

options.B: Function name, return type and parameters

mcqs.question: The statement that sends value back to calling function is:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse function declaration with function definition
Forgetting to write return statement in functions that return values
Not matching parameter types in function declaration and definition
Writing function call without proper arguments
Confusing built-in functions with user-defined functions in examples
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question in every BSEK paper
Always memorize the syntax of function declaration and definition
Practice writing complete C programs with user-defined functions
Remember to differentiate between declaration and definition with examples
formulas: Function definition syntax: return_type function_name(parameter_list) { statements; return value; }
note_data.keyDiagram: Function structure diagram showing: Function Declaration → Function Definition → Function Call → Return Value flow. Also draw the complete structure of C program with user-defined function including #include, function declaration, main() function, and function definition.
